# AI-code-video

Electron + React + TypeScript desktop app that turns YAML scripts into vertical (9:16) exam-prep short videos. Pipeline: ElevenLabs TTS (with per-word timings) → **deterministic code-built scene HTML** → Hyperframes renders HTML→MP4 → FFmpeg assembles with burned-in karaoke captions, transitions, and background music.

**This is a fork of the "AI Video Creator" app.** The one architectural difference: the three MIDDLE scenes are built DETERMINISTICALLY in code and synced to the voiceover, instead of being generated by Claude on every render. That removes the loops / empty boxes / sparse-frame failures the AI path produced. The user A/B-tested both and **chose this deterministic system (2026-07)** — continue here, not in the parent app.

## Run
- `npm install`, then `npm run dev` (electron-vite). **Windows is the primary target** (macOS secondary) — keep everything cross-platform.
- Shares the same Electron app name (`ai-video-creator`) as the parent app, so it reads the **same `userData`**: settings, API keys, voice profiles, music, and template backdrops at `userData/template-assets/<channel-slug>/set-<N>/<card>_bg.png`. Don't run both apps at once (shared job queue / settings).
- `npm run typecheck` — expect exactly 2 pre-existing harmless errors (ffprobe-static missing types; one implicit-any in `claude.ts:1800`). Anything else is new.

## Pipeline (`src/main/pipeline/`)
- `tts.ts` — ElevenLabs Turbo v2 via `/with-timestamps`. `WordTiming[]` (per-word start/end seconds) powers BOTH the captions and the scene reveal-sync.
- `runner.ts` — per-segment orchestration (TTS → build HTML → Hyperframes render → motion audit → assemble). Middle-scene routing: `spec.question` → `buildQuestionSceneHtml`; else per-scene `scene.chart` → `buildChartSceneHtml`; else → `buildSyncedSceneHtml`; the Claude `generateSceneHtml` is now only an emergency fallback. Includes the **near-empty backstop** (swap a blank/box-only final frame to the static card).
- **`syncedscenes.ts`** — deterministic CONCEPT scenes. Parses the explainer into a scene type (plain bands / text box / marks+star), draws it in code, and anchors each line's fade-in to the moment its phrase is spoken. One-pass reveals (`animation-fill-mode:both`, one iteration) → physically cannot loop or end empty. Exports `buildPalette`, `esc`, `norm`, `sizeFor`, `Palette` (reused by the quiz renderer).
- **`questionscenes.ts`** — deterministic QUESTION-SHORT quiz scenes: scene 0 = question + lettered options revealed one-by-one + "Guess the answer" + a **5-4-3-2-1 countdown synced to the spoken count**; scene 1 = correct option highlighted (green + check) + explanation; scene 2 = why each wrong option is wrong, one at a time.
- **`chartscenes.ts`** — deterministic CHART/DIAGRAM scenes: when a `SceneSpec` carries an optional `chart:` block, that middle scene is drawn in code (SVG/HTML) instead of text bands. Four types — `bar` (horizontal bars from `data:[{label,value}]`), `compare` (two-column table from `headers` + `rows:["left | right"]`), `flow` (step boxes + down-arrows from `steps:[…]`), `donut` (proportion ring + legend from `data`). Reuses `buildPalette`/`computeReveals`/`esc`/`sizeFor` from syncedscenes, so each bar/row/step/segment reveals in sync with the voiceover; one-pass animations (can't loop/empty); runs through the same `fitHtmlToSafeZone`. Claude chooses per concept whether to emit a chart (guidance in `buildScriptPrompt`); `parseChart` validates the data and the factory validator exempts chart scenes from the display-line/band/star rules.
- **`docdetect.ts`** — `detectDocType()` routes a raw upload to `questions` / `storyboard` / `concepts`; `parseQuestions()` parses a question bank deterministically (answer stays exact).
- **Factory queue (ipc.ts `FACTORY_GENERATE`):** submitting a doc only ENQUEUES it — `processFactoryDoc` runs in the BACKGROUND, one doc at a time (`factoryQueue` + `pumpFactoryQueue`), so the New Job UI never blocks and you can queue multiple documents (any channel) back-to-back. Progress streams over `PREVIEW_EVENT` (prefixed with the exam name); render jobs are created as scripts pass and the render worker renders in parallel. The renderer's factory buttons gate on a brief local `fSubmitting`, not the streaming status. **The `PREVIEW_EVENT` subscription lives in `App.tsx` (not `NewJobPage`) so factory progress keeps updating across tab switches — `App` owns the `preview` status and shows a persistent sidebar indicator; `NewJobPage` receives `preview`/`setPreview` as props.**
- `factory.ts` — raw doc → verified scripts. **Full-exam BATCH planner** (`planExamBatch` + pure `normalizeBatchPlan`, `DEFAULT_BATCH_TARGETS` = 3 theory + 2 bar + 2 compare + 1 flow + 1 donut + 2 questions = 11): a theory/concept upload becomes a planned MIX — Claude assigns each concept a best-fit chart type (**match-by-fit; never forces a type onto a concept lacking real data — chart counts flex, theory back-fills to keep 15**) and AUTHORS the questions; each authored answer is independently re-checked by `verifyQuestionAnswer` (fresh Claude that never sees the marked answer) and dropped unless it agrees + is confident. Concept top-up: `expandConcepts()` writes extra exam concepts to fill the pool. Chart scenes are forced via `buildScriptPrompt`'s `forceChart` option. Question banks (docType `questions`) keep the exact-answer path (`buildQuestionPrompt` + `validateQuestionScript`). Storyboards: `normalizeStoryboardToConcepts` → concept path → batch planner. If the planner call fails, falls back to a plain theory batch. Concept scripts still pass `validateGeneratedScript` + `reviewScriptWithClaude`.
- `cards.ts` / `storycards.ts` — deterministic intro/outro cards (badge = `exam_name`, word-by-word hook, subscribe pill, self-drawing arrow) + per-channel backdrop template sets (10 per channel).
- `safezone.ts` — measure DOM vs the 9:16 safe area, then a universal translate+scale fit. `captions.ts`, `ffmpeg.ts`, `motion.ts`, `parser.ts`.

## Script shape (`src/shared/types.ts` → `ScriptSpec`)
- **Concept short:** `intro` + `outro` (each has scene1/scene2 + voiceover) + exactly 3 `scenes` (explainer + voiceover + transition_out). The 3-scene pattern: scene 1 plain text bands / scene 2 a text box / scene 3 marks (✓ ✓ ✗) + one FILLED ★ memory hook. Display lines are quoted, each on its own line, ≤ ~48 chars. The exam name shows on the intro badge and must never appear in scene1.
- **Question short:** add a top-level `question:` block — `ask`, `options` (2–4), `correct` (0-based index), `explain`, `wrong[]` (aligned to options, blank at the correct index). The 3 scenes supply the quiz voiceovers; **scene-1's voiceover must end "…five, four, three, two, one"** so the on-screen countdown syncs.
- **Chart scene (optional):** any middle scene may add a `chart:` block to render a code-drawn visual instead of text bands: `type` (`bar`/`compare`/`flow`/`donut`) + `title` + type-specific data (`data:[{label,value}]` for bar/donut; `headers`+`rows:["left | right"]` for compare; `steps:[…]` for flow). Prefer at most one chart, on scene 2, keeping scene 1 the text hook and scene 3 the marks+star. A chart scene still needs `explainer` + `voiceover` (reveals sync to the voiceover) but no quoted display lines / bands / star.
- Inline emphasis in a concept explainer: `"gives" in blue and "takes" in coral`. Colors: exactly 5 hex (bg, ink/off-white, + 3 accents); `buildPalette` classifies them by hue. Fonts: a hand font + a sans.

## Conventions & gotchas
- All overlays clamp into the safe zone; never disable the clamps.
- Standalone logic tests: bundle a module with `esbuild --format=cjs`, aliasing `electron` to a tiny stub (safezone imports electron) and `@shared` to `src/shared`, then run with node. See the scratchpad tests in prior chats.
- `git push` needs the sandbox disabled in the Claude Code environment here. The Desktop volume is iCloud-synced, so git/file ops are slow.
- Example test scripts (in `~/Downloads`): `OAG_D429_Function_vs_Program.yaml` (concept), `PA_RealEstate_Joint_Tenancy.yaml` (concept), `PA_RealEstate_Question_Variance.yaml` (question). Drop one as a job and render.

## Status / next
- **Done:** synced concept scenes; question shorts; auto-detecting factory (question bank / storyboard / concepts); near-empty backstop; deterministic chart scenes (bar/compare/flow/donut, `chartscenes.ts`); **full-exam batch planner** — a theory doc yields an 11-video mix (3 theory + 2 bar + 2 compare + 1 flow + 1 donut + 2 authored+verified questions), match-by-fit; **background factory queue** — multiple documents can be queued without blocking the UI. Logic covered by esbuild+node tests: 62-assertion chart suite + 20-assertion batch-planner suite. Live Claude planner/verify calls + MP4 renders NOT yet exercised in-repo (need the user's keys + Hyperframes).
- **Channels:** State Exams Prep and OA Guides each have all 10 template sets measured. OA Practice + Nursing template packs are pending the user's storyboards.
- **Not verified in-repo:** live Claude prompt output and actual MP4 renders (need the user's ElevenLabs + Anthropic keys and Hyperframes). Logic is covered by esbuild+node tests.
